output:
The output contains at least 3 URLs starting with https:// and their associated text snippets containing password security recommendations:

1. https://www.isaca.org/certification/cism
2. https://www.adselfserviceplus.com/
3. https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/entertainment/entra-id

tool_calls.result:
Search results for 'password security best practices 2026':
1. Master essential IT security techniques by earning your CISM certification from ISACA. Stand out in the job market with a CISM certification and elevate your career!
2. Discover modern password security best practices for 2026. Learn human-centric strategies like passphrases & MFA to stop data breaches without employee burnout.
3. The latest updates in NIST Special Publication shift focus from complexity to usability. Key changes include: 1. Prioritizing password length over complexity. 2. Mandating compromised credential screening. 3. Encouraging passwordless authentication methods. 4. Eliminating forced password resets unless compromise is suspected.
4. Changing password policies requires updating documentation, retraining users, and potentially modifying multiple systems. Misperception of risk: Some security teams worry that removing complexity requirements or expiration periods will be seen as weakening security, even though the evidence suggests the opposite. Questions for Your Organization
5. Password security best practices for 2026. Password length vs complexity, managers, MFA, passkeys, NIST guidelines, and secure password generation.

Whether you're modernizing legacy systems or building new applications, our team provides the guidance and tools necessary to implement industry-leading security practices.
3. Learn the top password best practices for 2026. From creating strong passwords to utilizing MFA and password managers, learn how to safeguard your passwords and prevent common security blunders.
4. The latest updates in NIST Special Publication shift focus from complexity to usability. Key changes include: 1. Prioritizing password length over complexity. 2. Mandating compromised credential screening. 3. Encouraging passwordless authentication methods. 4. Eliminating forced password resets unless compromise is suspected.
5. Industry technical experts field-tested and stated the best password management best practices that your teams and enterprises can follow in 2026 to balance security and operational efficiency.

Search results for 'passwordless authentication methods 2026':
1. Passwords are responsible for 80% of data breaches. Passwordless authentication eliminates the attack surface entirely. Here is the complete technical and business guide to how it works, which methods fit which scenarios, and how to implement it in 2026.
2. The top passwordless authentication solutions for the workforce in 2026 are Microsoft Entra ID, ADSelfService Plus, Duo, and more. Explore the full list.
3. Explore passwordless authentication, passkeys, push MFA, device trust, and continuous authentication shaping secure and seamless login experiences in 2026.
4. The passwordless authentication landscape in 2026 sits at an inflection point. Passkeys have achieved what the security industry failed to do for 20 years: a password replacement that is simultaneously more secure and more convenient.
5. Discover how emerging authentication technologies are transforming security practices and learn how to implement passkeys and WebAuthn for a safer digital future.

Search results for 'NIST guidelines password security 2026':
1. The guidelines are not intended to constrain the development or use of standards outside of this purpose. This publication supersedes NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-63B. Keywords authentication; authentication assurance; credential service provider; digital authentication; passwords. Preface
2. In August 2024, the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) released Special Publication 800-63B Revision 4, updating their digital identity guidelines with significant changes to password requirements.
3. The latest updates in NIST Special Publication shift focus from complexity to usability. Key changes include: 1. Prioritizing password length over complexity. 2. Mandating compromised credential screening. 3. Encouraging passwordless authentication methods. 4. Eliminating forced password resets unless compromise is suspected.
4. The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) has released updated guidelines for password security, marking a significant shift from traditional password practices.
5. A comprehensive summary of the NIST password guidelines for 2026. Discover why mandatory expiration is out, length is in, and how to update your security policy with PassHulk.
